steps.explanation: Distribute $9$ across both terms inside the parentheses: $$9(x^2 + 6) = 9 \cdot x^2 + 9 \cdot 6 = 9x^2 + 54$$

Evaluate all choices against the result $9x^2 + 54$:
  • A: $9x^2 + 3$ — This would require $9 \times 6 = 3$, which is false. Incorrect.
  • B: $9x^2 + 6$ — This leaves the constant term undistributed ($6$ instead of $54$). Incorrect.
  • C: $9x^2 + 15$ — This would require $9 \times 6 = 15$, which is false. Incorrect.
  • D: $9x^2 + 54$ — $9 \times 6 = 54$, so this matches the distributed form exactly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
